413.reason: The greedy pattern for reserved words uses a negative lookahead `(?![_A-Za-z0-9])` to ensure they are not followed by identifier characters. However, this lookahead does not account for the end of line or end of file. When a reserved word like `main` appears at the end of a line (e.g., `int main()`), the lookahead fails because the next character is a newline, which is not matched by `[_A-Za-z0-9]`, but the pattern is applied to the substring of the current line only. In `FileLexer.matchTheStr`, the pattern is matched against `getLineRest()`, which returns the rest of the current line. If the reserved word is at the end of the line, the lookahead will try to match the next character, but since `getLineRest()` only contains the rest of the line, the lookahead will fail because there is no character after the word, causing the pattern to not match. This results in the reserved word not being recognized and instead being parsed as an identifier.

14.reason: This line will pass null inputStream if file not found, leading to NullPointerException in FileLexer constructor (scanLines). The program does not exit or handle the error properly.

302.reason: [No handling for unrecognized characters; if no token pattern matches, the position is not advanced, causing an infinite loop in doLexer]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [No handling for unrecognized characters; if no token pattern matches, the position is not advanced, causing an infinite loop in doLexer]

371.reason: The regex pattern `'.'` only matches single characters between quotes (e.g., 'a'). It fails to match escape sequences like '\0' (which consists of '\' and '0') or any multi-character content. When the lexer encounters '\0', no token pattern matches the starting quote, causing findToken() to return without advancing the position, resulting in an infinite loop in doLexer().

253.reason: If findToken() does not match any token and fails to advance the fileLexer position, this loop will become infinite, causing Time Limit Exceeded. This happens because the CHRCON regex "\\'.\\'" cannot match multi-character char constants like '\0' (which has two chars between quotes: backslash and 0), and no other token type matches the single quote character, leaving the lexer stuck at the same position.

402.reason: [The pattern "&" will match the first '&' in "&&", causing incorrect error detection because the lexer checks token types in enum order and may match SINGLEAND before AND.]

403.reason: [Similarly, the pattern "\\|" will match the first '|' in "||", leading to false error reporting for valid OR tokens.]
